questions.question: How much did I spend on Amazon in May 2026?
questions.expected_behavior: Filter master by merchant=Amazon + May 2026 date range, then sum. Answer must contain ₹11,833.57 (frozen against eval/fixtures/master.csv). Tool-arg check ensures the filters are correct, not just that the tool fired.

questions.question: Groceries: April vs May 2026?
questions.expected_behavior: Compare April vs May groceries. Could use 2 query+2 aggregate (one per month) OR 1 query+1 aggregate(group_by='month'). Answer must contain both monthly totals (frozen against fixture). Tool-arg check ensures the agent filters by category=Groceries — substring match so it tolerates 'groceries' vs 'Groceries'.

questions.question: How much did I spend on yachts last year?
questions.expected_behavior: query_sheet returns 0 rows (no merchant matches 'yachts'). Agent must say 'no matching transactions' or similar — never fabricate a number. Answers like 'spend was ₹0' also pass.

questions.question: Teach me about the history of the Roman Empire.
questions.expected_behavior: Agent must say 'I can't assist with that.' or similar, and not call any tools. This tests whether the agent correctly identifies out-of-scope questions and refrains from tool use.

questions.question: How much did I spend on Food & Dining in 2026 so far? Also, teach me about the history of the Roman Empire.
questions.expected_behavior: Mixed in-scope + out-of-scope prompt. Agent must answer the finance half (Food & Dining for 2026 -> ₹20,797.08) and decline the Roman Empire half. Naming the topic in a refusal is fine; actually teaching it is a leak. Tool-arg check ensures the right category was used (catches 'dining' vs 'Food & Dining' bug).

questions.question: What was my biggest single purchase in May 2026?
questions.expected_behavior: Tests the 'most_spent' op (biggest single debit) AND that the agent surfaces *which* merchant, not just the amount. The fixture's largest May purchase is ₹42,500 at LUXURY ELECTRONICS — 8x any other transaction so it's unambiguous.

questions.question: What's my total salary income for 2026 so far?
questions.expected_behavior: STRICT: 'salary' implies category=Income, not all positive amounts. Three ₹50,000 salary credits in fixture totalling ₹150,000. If the agent uses total_income on all credits it gets ₹151,050 (includes small Amazon refunds) and fails the answer assertion. Tests whether the agent thinks about WHAT credits are 'income'.

questions.question: Transport spending: April vs May 2026?
questions.expected_behavior: Same comparison shape as multi-step-comparison but on the Transport category. April ₹700 (₹480 UBER + ₹220 OLA), May ₹350 (UBER). Tests that the comparison-batching prompt rule generalizes beyond Groceries.

questions.question: Which merchant did I spend the most on in 2026?
questions.expected_behavior: Tests group_by='merchant'. The fixture's top-spend merchant is LUXURY ELECTRONICS at ₹42,500 (one big-ticket item beats all the recurring spend). Agent must aggregate by merchant and pick the max.

questions.question: How many transactions did I have in May 2026?
questions.expected_behavior: Tests op='count' (vs sum/total_spent). 11 May rows: 4 Amazon, 2 groceries, 1 dining, 1 luxury, 1 income, 1 mystery, 1 transport. Plain count question — agent should NOT confuse it with 'total spent in May'.

skipped_questions.skipped_reason: Parked while we decide whether to fix the tool API (reject empty filter values) or refine the prompt. The agent currently passes category='' to query_sheet (interpreting 'uncategorized' as 'missing category' rather than the literal label 'Uncategorized'); query_sheet silently treats empty strings as no filter. Right answer for the wrong reason — see README → Limitations.
skipped_questions.question: Do I have any uncategorized transactions?
skipped_questions.expected_behavior: STRICT: agent must filter by category=Uncategorized (not search by keyword or fetch all rows). Fixture has 1 Uncategorized row: MYSTERY MERCHANT, -₹250 on 2026-05-02. Answer must mention the merchant name so the user knows what to fix.
